story: Meet Dezső Kosztolányi, a Hungarian whiz with words who was born in 1885. He loved playing with phrases and rhymes more than most kids love cake. Known as an author and poet, his work inspired many future writers - just like how your favorite book might inspire you! What's wild is he was so good at it, his words are still read and enjoyed today, more than 100 years later!

Imagine you're at the biggest party of the year, and the music starts playing. The singer steps up, and it's Braguinha, the Brazilian superstar born in 1907! He was much more than just a singer - he also wrote his own songs and created music that made people want to dance. With his catchy tunes, he produced some of the most famous samba songs ever, making everyone tap their feet to the irresistible beat!

Picture it: 1913, a little boy named Phil Foster is born who grows up to be an amazing American actor. Just like how you might pretend to be a superhero or a princess, Phil loved to perform, and he became so good at it that he starred in popular TV shows! And guess what? Some of these shows are still loved today, decades after they were first aired!

Grab a golf club, because in 1962, a future golf champion named Kirk Triplett is born. Now, remember how excited you get when you win a game at school? Triplett won golf tournaments around the world! Golf might seem slow and steady, but Kirk showed everyone how thrilling this sport can be when he won major championships!

You know how it feels when you share an exciting story with your friends? In 1927, John McLaughlin was born. He grew up to be an American journalist and producer, sharing big stories with millions of people around the world. He made history so fun and exciting that he became famous for his reporting. Now that's what we call making homework exciting!
